title: Use cases
lead: LetterLab is built for real communication: short messages, important requests, and professional outreach. Pick a scenario below to see how the workflow fits.

seo.title: Use Cases | LetterLab
seo.description: How LetterLab fits students, job seekers, and teams.

items.title: Students
items.overview: Write clearer messages to professors, advisors, and classmates—without overthinking tone.

samples.situation: Requesting an extension on a paper
samples.sampleEmail:
Subject: Extension request – [Course] paper

Dear Professor [Name],

I am writing to request a short extension on the [assignment name] paper, currently due [date]. [One sentence reason, e.g., unexpected illness.] I can submit by [proposed date]. Please let me know if that works.

Thank you,
[Your name]

samples.situation: Asking for clarification on an assignment
samples.sampleEmail:
Subject: Clarification – [Assignment]

Hi Professor [Name],

I have a quick question about [assignment]: [state question]. I’ve checked [syllabus/announcements] but couldn’t find the detail. Could you clarify when convenient?

Thanks,
[Your name]

items.title: Job seekers
items.overview: Reach out professionally to recruiters and mentors with confident, respectful tone.

keyPoints: One specific point from the conversation
samples.sampleEmail:
Subject: Thank you – [Role] interview

Dear [Name],

Thank you for taking the time to speak with me today about the [role] position. I enjoyed learning about [specific topic from conversation] and am very interested in joining the team.

Please let me know if you need anything else from my side.

Best regards,
[Your name]

Subject: Quick question about [Topic/Role]

Hi [Name],

I’m [one line about you]. I’m reaching out because [reason]. Would you have 15 minutes for a short call in the next couple of weeks? I’d appreciate any advice you can share.

Thank you,
[Your name]

items.title: Teams
items.overview: Keep internal communication short, structured, and action-oriented.

samples.sampleEmail:
Subject: Follow-up – [Meeting name] [Date]

Hi all,

Quick follow-up from today’s meeting:

• Decision: [brief summary]
• Action items: [Name] – [task] by [date]; [Name] – [task] by [date]
• Next sync: [date/time]

Reply with any corrections or additions.

Thanks,
[Your name]

Subject: Status – [Project] [Date]

Hi [Name/team],

Update for this week: [1–2 sentences on progress]. [One line on blocker if any.] Next: [one clear next step].

[Your name]
